narrative: The FY2022-23 benchmark is led by Tata on breadth of supply-chain due diligence and local sourcing, while SAIL leads only on MSME/small-producer sourcing. Tata ranked 1st on value-chain environmental assessment at 78%, 1st on sustainable sourcing at 100% tied with JSW and SAIL, and 1st on local sourcing at 41%; SAIL’s 32.71% MSME sourcing narrowly beat Tata’s 32%. Jindal Stainless was weaker on sustainable sourcing at 90% and did not report value-chain environmental assessment, while JSW had low MSME sourcing at 5%, zero environmental assessment and very low local sourcing at 2.8%. Tata’s FY2022-23 data, however, is consolidated and includes Indian and international operations, so the apparent leadership should not be read as purely India-standalone outperformance. Overall, Tata’s position reflects a more mature responsible sourcing architecture, while SAIL’s MSME result reflects public-sector procurement obligations and vendor-development orientation.
